channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: A Hong Kong zoo seeks answers after 9 monkeys die in 2 days
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Hong Kong's oldest zoo is seeking answers in a monkey medical mystery after nine animals died in two days, including three members of a critically endangered species.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hereditary nobles have sat in Britain's Parliament for centuries. Their time may be up
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Like his ancestors for centuries, the Earl of Devon serves in Parliament, helping to make the laws of the land. But not for much longer.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Greek official accuses EU of policy failure on migration as war and climate change fuel displacement
item.description: A Greek official has criticized the outgoing European Commission for failing to forge a common policy to deport migrants and warned that war and climate change were increasing global displacement.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Conkers controversy: World tournament investigates claims of cheating with steel chestnut
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The World Conker Championships said Tuesday that it was investigating allegations of cheating after this year's men’s winner was found to have a steel chestnut in his pocket.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Southern Africa is enduring its worst hunger crisis in decades due to El Niño, the UN says
item.description: The United Nations' food agency says months of drought in southern Africa triggered by the El Niño weather phenomenon have had a devastating impact on more than 27 million people and caused the region’s worst hunger crisis in decades.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Taliban-run media stops showing images of living beings in some Afghan provinces
item.description: A Taliban official says official media have stopped showing images of living beings in some Afghan provinces to comply with morality laws.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How did a killing at a Sikh temple lead to Canada and India expelling each other's diplomats?
item.description: Relations between India and Canada are at a low point as the countries expelled each other’s top diplomats over an ongoing dispute about the killing of a Sikh activist in Canada.
item.dc:creator: Sheikh Saaliq, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Father called UK police to confess to killing daughter, 10, in England after he fled to Pakistan
item.description: The father of a 10-year-old girl who was found dead in England called U.K. police, after flying to Pakistan, to say that he had killed his daughter, prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Campaigning begins for Japan's parliamentary election
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Official campaigning for Japan's Oct. 27 parliamentary election began Tuesday with new Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba seeking a mandate for his policies and for reforms after the governing party’s political funds scandal.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea blows up parts of inter-Korean road and rail links in a symbolic display of anger
item.description: South Korea says North Korea blew up the northern parts of inter-Korean road and rail links no longer in use.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UN Security Council voices 'strong concern' for UN peacekeepers after Israeli attacks
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. Security Council expressed “strong concern” Monday as Israel has fired on and wounded U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon during intensified fighting, reiterating its support for their role in supporting security in the region. It's the first statement by the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hezbollah's drones are a fierce and evasive threat to Israel
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — One of the worst mass casualty strikes on Israel in a year of war came not from dozens of Iranian ballistic missiles nor the repeated barrages of rocket fire launched by Hamas and Hezbollah. Instead, it was a single drone.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vial that contained nerve agent that killed UK woman contained enough poison to kill thousands
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The lead counsel for a public inquiry into the 2018 death of a British woman poisoned by a Soviet-developed nerve agent said Monday that there was enough poison in the vial she unwittingly opened to kill thousands of people.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India-Canada diplomatic war intensifies over killing probe
item.description: India has strongly rejected Canada's allegations that its diplomats are "persons of interest" in a murder investigation, stating that the claims are part of Trudeau's "political agenda" and that Canada had failed to provide evidence to support its claims.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: UNIFIL ignored Hezbollah terror build up for 18 years, Israel's UN ambassador says
item.description: Amid fighting between Israel and Hezbollah terrorists, the U.N. is facing criticism that its peacekeeping force has failed to stop the Iran-terror proxy's military build up in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Koreans fighting in Ukraine alongside Russians, Zelensky says
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Sunday that North Korean troops are now fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine, appearing to confirm reports that have been circulating in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: UN peacekeepers in southern Lebanon are in the crosshairs of Israel’s war on Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — As the war between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah group escalates, a United Nations peacekeeping force in Lebanon is increasingly in the crosshairs, with Israeli troops firing at the peacekeepers' headquarters and positions several times in the past week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea is preparing to destroy northern parts of inter-Korean roads, Seoul says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea said Monday it has detected signs that North Korea is preparing to destroy the northern parts of inter-Korean roads no longer in use, as the rivals are embroiled in soaring tensions over North Korea’s claim that South Korea flew drones over its territory.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pentagon will send advanced missile defense system, military personnel to Israel
item.description: The U.S. will send to Israel a missile defense system and military personnel to operate it, the Pentagon announced Sunday. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin authorized the deployment of a Terminal High-Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) battery "and associated crew of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, The Hill

item.title: Trump touts relationship with Kim Jong Un, Putin
item.description: Former President Trump on Sunday touted his relationships with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Indian politician with Bollywood ties shot dead in Mumbai weeks before key state election
item.description: Baba Siddique, 66, a senior politician in India’s financial capital Mumbai, has been fatally shot weeks before a key state election, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Columbus remains, verified after 500 years, show he was Jewish: documentary
item.description: A 22-year investigation by Spanish forensic researchers concluded that Christopher Columbus was a Sephardic Jew from Western Europe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Scottish leader Alex Salmond, who nearly got Scotland's independence from UK, dies at 69
item.description: Alex Salmond, the former first minister of Scotland and a prominent advocate for Scottish independence, has died at the age of 69 in Ohrid, North Macedonia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-NATO chief warns Iran-Israel conflict could 'shut down oil coming out of Middle East'
item.description: James Stavridis, former NATO Chief Admiral, joined the Cats Roundtable radio show Sunday morning to weigh in on the impact of extreme conflict in Israel. The former leader said the rising tensions in the region “could […] shut down oil coming out of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Biden: Israel should 'absolutely' stop strikes on UN peacekeepers in Lebanon
item.description: President Biden said Friday that Israel should “absolutely” stop strikes on the U.N. peacekeepers in Lebanon, following two incidents the group was involved in this week.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Israeli forces kill 2 Lebanese soldiers and injure 2 UN peacekeepers in separate strikes
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — An Israeli airstrike killed two Lebanese soldiers and wounded three on Friday, Lebanon's military said, just hours after the Israeli military fired on the headquarters of U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon, injuring two of them for the second day in a row.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize given to Japanese organization Nihon Hidankyo for its work against nuclear weapons
item.description: The Nobel Peace Prize was awarded Friday to Nihon Hidankyo, a Japanese organization of survivors of the U.S. atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, for its activism against nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER and ELENA BECATOROS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia launches 4th aerial attack in a week against Ukraine's grain-exporting Odesa region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A nighttime Russian missile strike on Odesa killed at least four people including a 16-year-old girl, regional authorities said Friday, in the latest in a series of attacks this week on the southern Ukrainian region that are likely intended to disrupt the country’s grain exports
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Archaeologists unearth well-preserved 4,000-year-old Egyptian tomb
item.description: A joint Egyptian-German archaeological mission found the tomb of an ancient governor's daughter. Two tombs were found, one inside the other, covered in intricate script and art.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel continues to weigh options, timing on Iran strike following Biden-Netanyahu call
item.description: President Biden and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu held a call that in part focused on Iran's recent missile attack on the Jewish state, and reportedly how Israel intends to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Archaeologists unearth unexpected find inside a tomb likely belonging to a Roman gladiator
item.description: In Turkey, archaeologists unearthed a tomb seemingly belonging to a Roman gladiator of the third century B.C., but were surprised to find the remains of 12 individuals inside.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: “We also sent equipment at the beginning of the pandemic,” Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said. “But about the phone calls — it’s not true.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: To cope with 'confrontational hysteria' by U.S. and South Korean forces, North Korea said it will block its border with South Korea permanently and will cut off railways and roads to the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fugitive father missing with 3 kids since 2021 filmed hiking in New Zealand
item.description: “This was a credible sighting, and police believe it was indeed Tom and his children,” a press release from New Zealand Police said.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Google loses final EU court appeal against 2.4 billion euro fine in antitrust shopping case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Google lost its final legal challenge on Tuesday against a European Union penalty for giving its own shopping recommendations an illegal advantage over rivals in search results, ending a long-running antitrust case that came with a whopping fine.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: FBI arrests Afghan man who officials say planned Election Day attack in the US
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI has arrested an Afghan man who officials say was inspired by the Islamic State militant organization and was plotting an Election Day attack targeting large crowds in the U.S., the Justice Department said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation
